summary: High‑energy early‑Dead set with scorching Lovelight and wild jams
blurb: The band roars through Turn On Your Lovelight and a snarling Good Morning Little School Girl, then rides a bluesy wave on Death Don't Have No Mercy. Highlights include New Potato Caboose, The Other One suite and a tight Alligator‑Caution sequence, with Pigpen’s organ and Mickey’s glockenspiel adding extra color.
review: On November 11, 1967 at the Shrine Exposition Hall the Grateful Dead delivered an electrifying early‑Dead performance that still feels fresh decades later. Jerry Garcia’s guitar is on fire—from a blistering take on Turn On Your Lovelight to a preternatural solo on Good Morning Little School Girl that sounds older than his years—while Pigpen’s organ swells under the psychedelic tide, especially on New Potato Caboose where Mickey Hart’s glockenspiel adds a quirky sparkle. The set flows with tight transitions: Cryptical Envelopment segues into The Other One and back again before launching into a funky New Potato Caboose, then bursts into an Alligator‑Caution jam that showcases the band’s raw, high‑energy vibe. Death Don't Have No Mercy lands as a soulful blues centerpiece, and the closing Caution keeps the momentum rolling. Across all four soundboard sources the show is consistently praised for its intensity, musicianship, and memorable moments, making this Shrine night one of the standout snapshots of the Dead’s 1967 evolution.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recordings are marked positive, repeatedly highlighting blistering guitar work, standout blues numbers, and tight transitions. Multiple sources praise the same songs (Lovelight, Death Don't Have No Mercy, New Potato Caboose) and note consistent high energy, indicating strong overall sentiment.

best_recording.reason: Good soundboard capture with crisp early tracks that showcase the key performances (Lovelight, Good Morning Little School Girl, Death Don't Have No Mercy) while remaining fully listenable throughout.

key_highlights: Scorching Lovelight guitar that drives the set forward
Powerful, blues‑laden Death Don't Have No Mercy
Funky New Potato Caboose featuring Mickey Hart's glockenspiel
Seamless Alligator‑Caution jam that caps the psychedelic momentum

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers blistering, preternatural guitar work—especially on Lovelight, the Schoolgirl riff and extended solos that sound older than his years.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart adds tasteful glockenspiel texture on New Potato Caboose, enhancing the psychedelic vibe.
